spec_match.rationale: The page does not instantiate or invoke SpeechRecognition at all, leaving the speech component completely unimplemented.

interactive.rationale: Has interactive workflow selectors and sample insert buttons, but the primary interaction (dictating sensitive notes via microphone) is completely mocked and lacks mic controls.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the parent blurb's description of a sensitive-note local triage app pattern conceptually, though the implementation is incomplete.

edge_cases.rationale: Speech recognition failure paths, mic access denials, and local recognition errors are not handled since the API is never called.

references.rationale: The page lacks a see also section and completely misses the required external chromestatus.com reference link.

design_system.rationale: Adheres to the design system layout using two panels, clear borders, and standard color variables.

openQuestions.title: Wire real SpeechRecognition dictation into the triage textarea
openQuestions.detail: The triage workflow has a text input field prefilled with support logs, but does not actually support dictating notes. Add a microphone trigger button next to the textarea that instantiates SpeechRecognition (using the ondevice option), updates the text input dynamically as the user speaks, and analyzes the dictated output in real-time.

openQuestions.title: Correct API naming in showcase code block
openQuestions.detail: The code snippet at the bottom of the page displays 'recognition.processLocally = true', which is inconsistent with other concepts that use 'recognition.ondevice = true'. Align this with the rest of the milestone demos to avoid developer confusion.

openQuestions.title: Add a see also section citing ChromeStatus
openQuestions.detail: Add the required 'see also' section at the bottom of the page with the external reference link to https://chromestatus.com/feature/6090916291674112 to fulfill the showcase quality rules.
